seo.title: Seedance 2.0 (ByteDance) — AI Video with Native Audio & Multi-Shot Control | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Explore ByteDance Seedance 2.0 on MaxVideoAI: native audio, 480p/720p output, auto or 4-15 second runs, multimodal references, realistic physics, and director-level camera control.

overview: Seedance 2.0 is ByteDance's latest and most advanced video generation model on Fal. It combines cinematic motion, multi-shot continuity, realistic physics, and native audio in a single generation.
pricingNotes: Seedance 2 uses Fal token pricing. MaxVideoAI adds its margin and rounds up to the next cent; Generate shows the final quote before submission.

hero.title: Seedance 2.0
hero.intro: Fal presents Seedance 2.0 as cinematic video with native audio, real-world physics, and director-level camera control. On MaxVideoAI, use it across text-to-video, image-to-video with an optional end frame, and reference-to-video with multimodal inputs when you need stronger camera grammar, action realism, and multi-shot continuity.
hero.badge: 480p/720p · Auto or 4-15s · Native audio · Text / image / reference modes

items.title: Action scenes with collisions, debris, and more believable physical interaction
Multi-shot ads with consistent product and scene continuity
Audio-led scenes with synced dialogue, ambience, and sound effects
Storyboard-to-video using text and multimodal references
Director-style camera moves, transitions, and framing control
Pre-visualization before final edit (concept to shot list to rough cut)

technicalOverview.label: Role
technicalOverview.body: ByteDance's latest video generation model with a unified multimodal audio-video architecture.

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text prompts, start image + optional end image, plus image/video/audio references in ref2v

technicalOverview.label: Reference limits
technicalOverview.body: Up to 9 images, 3 video clips, and 3 audio clips, with 12 total files across all ref2v inputs

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio is on by default and can be disabled; Fal states the price is the same either way

technicalOverview.label: Camera and motion
technicalOverview.body: Advanced camera control with strong results on dolly zooms, rack focus, tracking shots, POV switches, and smooth handheld movement

technicalOverview.label: Notes
technicalOverview.body: Fal's public page highlights text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, and optimized fast variants inside the Seedance 2 family.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Seedance 2.0
promptStructure.quote: [Genre] + [subject] + [setting] + [action beats] + [camera language] + [audio cues] + [duration auto or 4-15s] + [shot structure].
promptStructure.description: For best consistency, keep each beat explicit and define camera transitions and audio timing in the same prompt.
steps: Add dialogue, ambience, and SFX cues

promptingTip: Tip: Seedance 2.0 rewards shot timing, camera verbs, and audio cues. Keep dialogue short, pin SFX to visible actions, and use references to lock continuity.

tips.title: Production notes
items: Keep prompts structured shot-by-shot when you want the strongest multi-shot continuity.
Use multimodal references to lock wardrobe, props, pacing, and ambience across beats.
Use the optional end image in i2v when you need a guided last frame.
Move to Seedance 2.0 Fast when you need cheaper draft passes before final renders.
Generate shows the active route and the final quote before you submit.

prompts.title: Action chase (multi-shot)
prompts.prompt: Three-shot rooftop chase at dusk, shot 1 wide tracking run, shot 2 handheld close pursuit, shot 3 crane reveal over skyline, realistic footstep impacts and city sirens, 15 seconds total.

prompts.title: Continuous spy thriller
prompts.prompt: A spy exits a neon train station, camera follows over shoulder into a rainy alley, rack focus to a coded message, low synth score and distant PA announcements, 12 seconds.

prompts.title: 3-shot commercial
prompts.prompt: Shot 1 macro of watch crown with rim light, shot 2 wrist hero turntable in studio haze, shot 3 lifestyle close-up in city night rain, punchy transitions and polished ad soundtrack, 15 seconds.

prompts.title: Multimodal storyboard reference
prompts.prompt: Use @Image1..@Image9 as storyboard style anchors, @Video1..@Video3 for pacing references, @Audio1..@Audio3 for rhythm and ambience, keep wardrobe continuity and cinematic 16:9 framing.

prompts.title: Audio-forward dialogue scene
prompts.prompt: Two-character dialogue in a subway platform at night, synchronized dialogue, ambience and metallic train brakes on cue, smooth dolly and cut-in close-up, stereo mix feel, 10 seconds.

faqs.a: Seedance 2.0 is ByteDance's AI video model focused on cinematic motion, multi-shot continuity, and native audio generation.
Fal lists auto or 4 to 15 seconds per generation for the current Seedance 2.0 routes.
Yes. Native audio is available, generate_audio defaults to on, and Fal states pricing is the same whether audio is generated or not.

faqs.q: How should I check availability and pricing?
faqs.a: Open Generate to confirm the active route, supported workflows, and the final quote for your run before you submit.

faqs.q: Should I start with standard or Fast?
faqs.a: Start with standard Seedance 2.0 when you want the highest render quality, and switch to Seedance 2.0 Fast for quicker draft passes and comparison rounds.

custom.heroTrustLine: Pay-as-you-go on MaxVideoAI · Price shown before you generate

Real-world physics under pressure||Action scenes, collisions, debris, and fabric motion are part of the model's public positioning on Fal
Native audio in the same generation||Dialogue, ambience, music, and sound effects stay synchronized without separate audio layering
Unified multimodal control||Text, image, audio, and video inputs can be combined inside the Seedance 2 workflow family
Multi-shot outputs up to 15s||Fal highlights natural cuts and transitions inside a single generation rather than a single unbroken take

custom.promptingIntro: Seedance 2.0 tends to reward shot timing, camera verbs, and audio cues. Keep dialogue short, pin SFX to visible actions, and use references to lock continuity.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: Use auto duration when you want the model to decide pacing from the prompt.
Write camera movement and transition verbs clearly.
Keep one clear action beat per shot.
Pin dialogue and SFX to visible moments.
promptingEngineWhy: The model is optimized for cinematic camera language and controlled motion.
Multi-shot generation benefits from explicit beat structure.
Joint audio-video generation improves sync when audio cues are concise.
Reference-heavy workflows improve consistency across shots.

promptingTabNotes.structured: Structured = lock continuity and camera direction.
promptingTabNotes.pro: Pro = detailed multi-shot direction with sound design.

promptingTabs.label: Quick
promptingTabs.title: Quick concept prompt
promptingTabs.description: Fast ideation with one cinematic beat.
promptingTabs.copy:
[Subject] in [setting]. Camera: [move + lens feel]. Lighting: [style]. Action: [one clear beat]. Audio: [ambience + one SFX cue]. (Auto or 4-15s, choose aspect ratio in UI.)

Example: Handheld UGC unboxing at a kitchen table. Slow push-in, natural daylight. She peels the seal, smiles, turns the bottle to camera. Room tone + packaging crinkle + soft click when cap opens.

promptingTabs.label: Structured
promptingTabs.title: Structured production prompt
promptingTabs.description: Use this to keep consistency in multi-shot outputs.
promptingTabs.copy:
Continuity anchors: wardrobe, props, location, time of day
Shot timeline: 0-5s / 5-10s / 10-15s
For each shot: subject action + camera move + transition verb
Audio plan: ambience bed + timed SFX + short dialogue (optional)
Exclusions: avoid / do not include / safety boundaries

promptingTabs.label: Pro
promptingTabs.title: Director brief prompt
promptingTabs.description: Detailed cinematic control with synchronized sound design.
promptingTabs.copy:
Director intent (1 sentence):
Visual anchors:
Shot timeline + transitions:
Camera language: push-in / whip pan / crane / handheld / locked-off
Sound design: ambience, SFX hits, dialogue length
Constraints + negatives: no text overlays, no logos, no shaky cam, etc.

promptingTabs.label: Storyboard
promptingTabs.title: Storyboard multimodal prompt
promptingTabs.description: Reference-driven prompting with images, videos, and audio.
promptingTabs.copy:
Use @Image1..@Image9 to lock style and characters.
Use @Video1..@Video3 for pacing and camera rhythm.
Use @Audio1..@Audio3 for ambience texture.
Produce a coherent 3-shot output (auto or 4-15s) with explicit transition verbs.

strengths: Strong cinematic camera direction when beats are explicit.
Native audio sync in short multi-shot outputs.
High utility for ads, action beats, and storyboard prototyping.
troubleshootingItems: Cuts feel abrupt -> add transition verbs and timestamps (match cut at 5s, whip pan into Shot 2).
Continuity drifts -> add anchors (wardrobe, prop, location) and reuse references.
Audio mismatch -> shorten dialogue, pin SFX to visible action, keep one ambience bed.
Physics looks off -> simplify simultaneous actions per beat and reduce fast interactions.
boundaries: Complex action stacks still benefit from simpler, clearly separated beats.
Reference-heavy prompts work best when each source has one clear job.
Reference audio only works when at least one image or video reference is attached.
Check the active Fal route family before scaling production traffic.
Use the final quote in Generate before client-facing rollout or automated workflows.
